key_insight: A class-conditioned β-CVAE can generate Raman spectra that are useful for augmenting small glioma datasets and improving downstream classification.
clinical_relevance: This approach may help make Raman-based glioma biomarker classification more robust when patient data are scarce and imbalanced.
method: Built a conditional variational autoencoder to create synthetic Raman spectra by glioma class.
Tested binary IDH-status and 6-class methylation subtype classification.
Compared train-on-synthetic, train-on-synthetic-plus-real, and test-on-real under patient-isolated cross-validation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports 58 tumor samples and finds synthetic-only models underperform real-data models, while adding synthetic spectra consistently improves performance.
watch_item: There is a substantial domain gap between synthetic and real spectra, so gains depend on careful validation and may not transfer across acquisition settings.
paper_title: Generative Augmentation of Raman Spectra for Glioma Classification
